source_textbook.name: IB Mathematics: Analysis and Approaches SL/HL
source_textbook.section: Number, Functions, Trig, Vectors, Calculus, Statistics

extracted_concepts.name: arithmetic sequence
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A sequence with constant difference between terms

extracted_concepts.name: geometric sequence
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A sequence with constant ratio between terms

extracted_concepts.name: argument
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: arg(z) = arctan(b/a) for z = a + bi

extracted_concepts.name: polar form
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: z = r(cos θ + i sin θ)

extracted_concepts.name: asymptote
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A line that a curve approaches but never reaches

extracted_concepts.name: discriminant
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Δ = b² - 4ac determines nature of roots

extracted_concepts.name: vector
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A quantity with magnitude and direction

extracted_concepts.name: implicit differentiation
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Differentiating equations not solved for y

extracted_concepts.name: inverse function
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: f⁻¹ reverses the effect of f

extracted_concepts.name: binomial distribution
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Distribution of successes in n independent trials

extracted_concepts.name: normal distribution
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Bell-shaped distribution defined by μ and σ

extracted_relations.evidence: Both are special types of sequences
Modulus measures magnitude of complex number
Argument gives direction of complex number
Polar form uses modulus and argument
Dot product is fundamental vector operation
Implicit differentiation finds derivatives of implicit functions
Binomial is a specific probability distribution
Normal is the most important continuous distribution
CI often based on normal distribution
